items.situation_text: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths

44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.

decision_question: Given one available resource right now, who should receive it first?

A.source_text: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths
A.short_term: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths, immediate medical intervention is required to stabilize the child.

B.source_text: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.
B.short_term: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. The mangled, dirty extremity is in a bag, detached distal to the elbow. No tourniquet is in place, and she is bleeding profusely everywhere. She appears unsteady, with capillary refill at five seconds. Immediate action is required to stop the bleeding and stabilize her condition.
B.long_term: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. In the long run, without proper medical intervention, she will face significant challenges in regaining function and mobility in her upper body. Over time, she may require multiple surgeries, prolonged rehabilitation, and ongoing physical therapy. Months from now, she could experience chronic pain and limited range of motion. Eventually, she might need assistive devices to perform daily activities.
